clinical_information.diagnosis: Philadelphia chromosome-positive B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (Ph+ B-ALL), relapsed/refractory
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Induction (4/1/24-4/29/24): vincristine weekly, dexamethasone daily x28 days, L-asparaginase alternate days, single dose daunorubicin week 1, imatinib daily. Consolidation (5/6/24-7/1/24): high-dose methotrexate weekly, mercaptopurine daily, vincristine biweekly, cyclophosphamide week 3, doxorubicin final week, imatinib continued. Maintenance started 7/8/24: weekly methotrexate, daily mercaptopurine, vincristine every 4 weeks, imatinib daily; intrathecal methotrexate planned for 10/4/24. Bone marrow 3/29/24 showed 85% lymphoblasts at diagnosis. Repeat bone marrow 10/4/24 after consolidation shows >90% blasts indicating treatment failure/relapsed or refractory disease.
clinical_information.lab_summary: The most important abnormality is the bone marrow aspirate showing >90% blasts, which is markedly abnormal and meets criteria for acute leukemia (WHO threshold ≥20% blasts). The peripheral CBC values provided are mostly within reference ranges (WBC, differential percentages, hemoglobin, hematocrit, platelets normal). There is a mild basophilia (basophils 2%, reference 0–1%), which can be seen with certain myeloproliferative disorders or in some leukemias. Overall this pattern is highly suspicious for an acute leukemic process despite a relatively unremarkable peripheral CBC. Urgent hematology/oncology evaluation is indicated with prompt flow cytometry (marrow and/or peripheral blood), cytogenetic and molecular testing, peripheral smear review, and baseline chemistry/coagulation studies (CMP, uric acid, LDH, coagulation) to guide diagnosis and initial management (including tumor lysis precautions and supportive care).
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Patient is a 7-year-old male who is overall well-appearing with mild fatigue and decreased energy; no recent fevers or other systemic symptoms reported. Despite relatively mild symptoms, marrow disease burden is very high (>90% blasts).
clinical_information.prognosis_and_risk_if_not_approved: Relapsed/refractory Ph+ B-ALL with >90% marrow blasts carries high risk of rapid clinical deterioration including progressive marrow failure (severe cytopenias), life-threatening infection and bleeding, potential CNS or extramedullary spread, and poor overall survival without prompt effective salvage therapy. Delay in effective therapy may preclude curative options such as hematopoietic stem cell transplant.
clinical_information.clinical_rationale_for_urgency: Repeat marrow on 10/4/24 demonstrates >90% blasts despite standard induction/consolidation and ongoing imatinib, confirming relapsed/refractory disease. Immediate initiation of an effective salvage agent (requested blinatumomab) is needed to reduce tumor burden, prevent rapid progression/complications, and allow for bridging to potential curative therapy (e.g., transplant).

treatment_request.duration: Induction cycle 1 (Days 1-28); duration based on clinical response
treatment_request.rationale: Diagnosis of B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL). Patient has received 1 standard induction and consolidation cycle and is now in maintenance phase; request for Blincyto as indicated.

Clinical Policy: Blinatumomab (Blincyto)  
Reference N umber: ERX.SPA. 241 
Effective Date: 09 .01.18 
Last Review Date: 08.18  Revision Log  
 
See Important Reminder  at the end of this policy for important regulatory and legal information.  
 
Description 
Blinatumomab (Blincyto®) is a bispecific CD19- directed CD3 T -cell engager that binds to CD19  
(expressed on cells of B -lineage origin) and CD3 (expressed on T cells).  
 
FDA Approved Indication(s) 
Blincyto is indicated for :  
• MRD -positive B -cell precursor ALL  
o Treatment of B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in first or second complete 
remission with minimal residual disease (MRD) greater than or equal to 0.1% in adults and 
children. This indication is approved under accelerated approval based on MRD response rate and hematological relapse- free survival. Continued approval for this indication may be contingent 
upon verification and description of clinical benefit in the confirmatory trials.  
• Relapsed or refractory B -cell precursor ALL  
o Treatment of relapsed or refractory B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in adults 
and children.  
 
Policy/Criteria 
P

B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in adults 
and children.  
 
Policy/Criteria 
Provider must submit documentation (such as office chart notes, lab results or other clinical information) 
supporting that member has met all approval criteria.  
 
It is the policy of health plans affiliated with Envolve Pharmacy Solutions™ that Blincyto is  medically 
necessary when the following criteria are met:  
 I. Initial Approval Criteria  
A. Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (must meet all) : 
1. Diagnosis of B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B -ALL);  
2. Prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist  or hematologist ; 
3. Requested as treatment for (a or b):  
a. B-ALL in remission but positive for minimal residual disease (MRD+);  
b. Relapsed or refractory B -ALL (i and ii):  
i. Philadelphia chromosome- negative (Ph- ) disease;  
ii. Philadelphia chromosome- positive (Ph+) disease and intolerant or refractory to at 
least one second- generation or later tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI; i.e., Sprycel
®, 
Tasigna®, Bosulif®, Iclusig®); 
*Prior authorization is (or may be) required for these agents  
4. Dose does  not exceed 28 mcg/day.  
Approval duration:

decision.reason: Patient is a pediatric relapsed/refractory Ph+ B-ALL case and the requesting provider and dose meet policy elements, but the policy requires that for Ph+ relapsed/refractory B-ALL the patient be intolerant or refractory to at least one second-generation or later TKI (e.g., Sprycel, Tasigna, Bosulif, Iclusig). The record documents prior imatinib (a first-generation TKI) but does not document trial and failure/intolerance of a second-generation or later TKI. Per the policy excerpt below, this missing information is required for approval.
